title: Rate limit improvements
excerpt:
We have made a handful of improvements to our rate limits to help maintain a high-quality API and support optimal performance. Some of these updates include: 

Standardized error codes: Rate limit errors will now return …

posts.author: rachelatmonday (Rachel)
posts.cookedHtml:
We have made a handful of improvements to our rate limits to help maintain a high-quality API and support optimal performance. Some of these updates include:

  1. Standardized error codes: Rate limit errors will now return a 429 HTTP error code to help standardize responses and simplify error handling.
  2. Retry-After header: Errors will now include the Retry-After header to indicate how long you need to wait before making another request.
  3. New rate limits: We’ve introduced new limits, including per-minute request limits and a concurrency limit. These changes help reduce the frequency of complexity limits, giving you more control over your API usage and providing a smoother developer experience.
  4. Daily limits: Daily request limits will be enforced for basic and standard accounts starting next week. Limits for pro and enterprise accounts will be gradually rolled out next year.

For more information, check out our rate limits documentation.

Drop your questions/comments below!

posts.author: Doka (Dominik Kukovec)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Rachel. Is there a point missing here?

  1. Daily limits: Daily request limits will be enforced for basic and standard accounts starting December 10th, 2024. Limits for pro and enterprise accounts will be gradually rolled out next year.
    (Rate limit improvements)

I have serious concerns regarding this as our automations are runnning throughout the whole day for various clients. What will the limit on those be? For enterprise and pro accounts? Will this be connected to the account_id or the API key?

posts.author: DanielHai (Daniel Hai)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dominik Kukovec:

account_id

Hi @Doka

The daily limit is associated with the account, it is there to prevent excessive use of the API.

At this time the daily limit for pro/enterprise accounts indicate the recommended usage.

Any changes to this policy will be communicated ahead of time.

posts.author: Doka (Dominik Kukovec)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Daniel.
Thank you, but I am not sure how the recommended usage for enterprise accounts
(in our case 400+ users) is 25k requests PER DAY.
For example:
If each of our users takes a look at their time logging board (hundreds of items) with pagination it gives about 100 requests per user, sometimes more, depends on the number of items in the boards. 100 * 400 = 40k. And that’s only if they take a look at the app once. But other than that we also have updating items, creating items, nightly processes that are reading and updating items, also in hundreds of items.

Who can we contact to address this?

posts.author: rachelatmonday (Rachel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Doka,

Good catch earlier and my apologies! I’ve edited the post to add it. We are using this thread to compile all feedback related to these limits!

Best,
Rachel

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: Why are we only getting notice of potentially impacting changes less than 24 hours notice before release?

posts.author: DanielHai (Daniel Hai)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dear API Users,

I want to provide some clarity on the recent announcement regarding the introduction of a daily API limit. I want to assure you that our primary objective is to ensure a smooth and seamless transition for all account, with no immediate impact on your current usage.

This new policy applies only to personal account API usage and not to marketplace app developers. For the time being, API usage by marketplace apps will not count towards this limit. Even when the limit is reached (and in cases where it is enforced), marketplace apps will remain unaffected.

For those accounts that will be impacted by this change, we have planned a two-step approach to ensure a smooth transition:

  1. We will provide a new observability dashboard for API usage, allowing you to monitor your usage and understand where the API is being utilized.

  2. If we anticipate any impact on your account due to this change, we will engage in personal communication and provide ample time for adjustments.

For our Pro and Enterprise tier users, this will be a soft limit for the foreseeable future to prevent any disruption to your workflows. If your account requires a higher API limit we will proactively adjust it based on the size and needs of the account.

Our ultimate goal remains unchanged - to continue providing an exceptional API experience that scales with your needs, and in the future is also appropriate for the tier and size of the account.

We appreciate your understanding and cooperation as we implement this new policy. As always, our technical support team is available to answer any questions or concerns you may have.

Happy Coding
Daniel Hai - API PM

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml: exclude_reply_from_sla

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
Daily limits do not apply to marketplace apps. This is good.

Question about the minute limit and concurrency limits. Are these per app / user token per account or are they simply per account regardless of the source? (meaning different apps are competing with each other for these resources)

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml: Checking with Daniel Slack

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @anon29275264,

The limits (both of them) are per app per account!

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks. I think the rate-limits documentation page needs improvement to clarify the scope of each limits.

There is even an interpretation that some limits aren’t per account. And an app can only make 500 mutations per minute globally to the platform, or x complexity per minute.

Complexity limit implies per user.
Daily limit implies per account.
Minute limit could be interpreted as account, app, or user.

This isn’t new to these recent changes. The complexity limits often raised the same questions - esp. the distinction between app tokens and personal tokens (I think personal tokens on an account actually all share the same complexity budget - do they also share the minute limit and concurrency limits?

Thank you again!

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello again @anon29275264 !

Thank you for the feedback! I have shared it with the team

The limit per minute and concurrency limit inside an account (no app involved) are per account (all users share it).

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: OpsPRMG (Henry J)
posts.cookedHtml:
Will there be new query objects for the new limits?
I’m thinking about something similar to the existing complexity object, so we can see where we are within each limit.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @OpsPRMG,

This is not going to be available in the near future but we will announce any changes!

posts.author: Nixoid (Nixoid)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, i have some additional question.

monday.com Platform API

Rate limits

Learn more about the monday.com platform API rate limits, calculating complexity, and timeout policies

All API calls made through personal tokens, private applications, and public applications (excluding marketplace apps and those developed by monday.com) count towards this limit.

What about others types of limits?
Are IP limit, Concurrency limit, Endpoint-specific minute limits, Minute limit affected to Marketplace App?

How it work if Marketplace App use personal users token for Api requests?

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @Nixoid,

  • IP limits are per IP address.
  • The concurrency limit is per app per account (if you use an app token, and then you use a personal token from an account, those two tokens do not share the quota for concurrent calls).
  • The minute limit is per app per account like the concurrency limit.
  • If you use a personal token, it counts as a personal token only as if no app was involved. It does not matter if it “comes from an app” because the actual HTTP request is made using an API personal token and it counts just as if the actual user did the call themselves.

Let me know if you have any other questions!

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: tjm (Truett Majors)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have what I thought was a very modest utilization of the monday.com Work management workspace supporting a business between myself and one other person. We pay for the standard plan. Approximately once a month via the API url, I need to load approximately 2000 items with 26 columns into a board. The load process is: 1) create a new item 2) then run a change_multiple_column_values query to load the column values.

There are approximately a dozen automations that are subsequently used very rarely in response to manual changes to a status value, ie 10 times a month to move items from one board to another. So after the initial load, there is very little activity within our monday.com workspace.

The new daily limit is tapping out this load process after about 500 items, meaning it takes from 4 to 5 days to complete the load.

In fact I’m only discovering this new daily limit now because I haven’t tried to load anything until recently.

Is there no way monday.com can accommodate this use case?

posts.author: rachelatmonday (Rachel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @tjm,

Welcome to the community!

Can you please open a ticket with our support team here so they can take a look at your account and see what solution we can find?

Best,
Rachel

posts.author: tjm (Truett Majors)
posts.cookedHtml: Done as requested.
